---
title: "getting-started"
description: >
  Basic usage of EmStanS for Embeded Standard Setting
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{getting-started}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval = F)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "vignettes",
  out.width = "100%"
)

options(tibble.print_min = 5, tibble.print_max = 5)
```

# EmStanS 

## Overview

This package aims to support Embedded Standard Setting, calculating counts and weights.


## Install


```{r}
devtools::install_github("sooyongl/EmStanS", dependencies = T)
```

## Usage

```{r eval = T}
library(EmStanS)
```

```{r eval = T}
# data <- read.csv(filePath)
fake_data <- genFakeDataSet(ngca = 3, 
               cor_val = 0.2,
               n = 30,
               nlevel = 3,
               ntable = 5,
               npanelist = 5,
               sdinp = 1,
               ecinp = 0,
               100,300)



fake_data

# Or you can use your own data structured by the following table.
```

```{r eval = T}
res <- emstans(data = fake_data,
               grade = c(1,2),
               targets = "ALD",
               WESS = T,
               gamest = F,
               median = "modal",
               loc = "Loc_RP50",
               domain = "GCA")

```


## Summary

```{r eval = T}
summary(res)
```


## Report

With `what = "all"`, the combined report is provided.

### Individual 

```{r eval = T}
report(res, what = "individual")
```

### Detailed 

```{r eval = T}
report(res, what = "detailed")
```


### Summary

```{r eval = T}
report(res, what = "summary")
```

### Item Review 

```{r eval = T}
report(res, what = "review")
```


## Extract

`extract()` provides the primary results of ESS as a form of `list()`.

```{r eval = T}
extract(res)
```
